poems.title: To Ask Her Name Again
poems.author: Ashu, Research Scholar of Economics, K.R Mangalam University Sohna Haryana
content: days at the needling needle, at the breast.
It was housework all in her labour.
clean floors, food that is cooked, folded life,
bad, one, who oversteps the boundaries.
time and children and home together,
for her labour to be counted,

poems.title: Closed Doors, Open Lies
poems.author: Andrea Mithra Rajan R, B.A. English, Chevalier T Thomas Elizabeth College for Women
content: Left or Right, Which is correct?
Pigs in power, Snakes in control.
And that’s why they wear coats.
From those in luxury and fashion.
Playing with fire, loving the flame,
Ignoring the burn, calling its name.
Who divide us with the past,
Then end us with their system.

poems.title: Suffering is not an Alien
poems.author: Jothika Rameshkumar, MA English, Madras Christian College, Madras University
content: Hope for the day, Without despair
I could hear the words without meaning
when it fails to produce without truth.
Agree! Our Complexity of emotions cannot be coherence ever!
Words do not have power until
it gives voice for my silly feelings
at the end they hold hands together
Agree! Our Complexity of emotions cannot be coherence ever!
Suffering is a part of emotions
often it unites me with the nature

poems.title: Existence
poems.author: Sharmila Jajodia, Research Scholar, Elphinstone College, Dr. Homi Bhabha State University
content: And in his search for existence
Like a living death, a death in life.
Everyman in search of the only way
That leads to his goal, his sole aim
As he wants to do something, someway.
No ray of light is with you.
The Inner Light is extinct too.

poems.title: Becoming After Rain
poems.author: Silja Sukumar, Research Scholar in English Literature, Karpagam Academy of Higher Education, Coimbatore
content: her dreams smelled like wet earth—
longing for a sky strong enough
She wandered through a forest of mist
mending the torn fabric of time.
She held the drops in her hands,
like a promise learning her name,
